feedback.dialog.title: Send feedback

@feedback.dialog.title.description: Title of the beta feedback dialog. Opened from the Help menu and other in-app prompts.

feedback.dialog.description: What''s working? What''s missing? Your note goes straight to the maker of Cmdr.

@feedback.dialog.description.description: Intro line at the top of the feedback dialog, inviting the user to share. Warm and direct. "Cmdr" is the product name (do not translate).

feedback.dialog.label: Your feedback

@feedback.dialog.label.description: Label above the multi-line text box where the user types their feedback.

feedback.dialog.placeholder: Example: I''d love a shortcut for jumping between tabs.

@feedback.dialog.placeholder.description: Placeholder text inside the empty feedback text box, giving an example of useful feedback.

@feedback.dialog.counter.description: Character counter shown near the feedback box once the text gets long. Both values are already-formatted numbers; render them as a fraction (typed so far over the maximum).

placeholders.currentText: number of characters typed so far, already formatted for display (e.g. 52,000)
placeholders.maxText: the maximum allowed character count, already formatted (e.g. 100,000)

feedback.dialog.tooLong: Sorry, that''s too long. Maximum is {maxText} characters.

@feedback.dialog.tooLong.description: Warning shown below the feedback box when the typed text exceeds the maximum length. Apologetic and brief.

placeholders.maxText: the maximum allowed character count, already formatted (e.g. 100,000)

feedback.dialog.attachEmail: Attach my email ({email}) so we can reply

@feedback.dialog.attachEmail.description: Label for an opt-in checkbox to include the user's email so the team can reply. Shown only when the user has an email on file; their actual address is shown in the parentheses.

placeholders.email: the user's email address on file (e.g. alex@example.com)

feedback.dialog.moreWays: You can also <github>browse and vote on GitHub</github> or <call>book a call</call> with David.

@feedback.dialog.moreWays.description: A line offering other ways to give feedback. <github> wraps a link to the GitHub issues page; <call> wraps a link to book a video call. "GitHub" is a brand name and "David" is the maker's name (do not translate either).

feedback.dialog.invalid: That note didn''t go through. Shorten it and try again?

@feedback.dialog.invalid.description: Shown when the server rejects the feedback as invalid (usually too long). Encourages the user to shorten and retry. Avoid the words "error" and "failed".

feedback.dialog.softFailure: Sorry, we couldn''t send your feedback right now. Try again?

@feedback.dialog.softFailure.description: Shown when sending the feedback didn't go through for a transient reason (e.g. network). Apologetic, suggests retrying. Avoid the words "error" and "failed".

@feedback.dialog.cancel.description: Button that closes the feedback dialog without sending. Keep it short (a tight button).

@feedback.dialog.send.description: Primary button that submits the feedback. Keep it short (a tight button).

@feedback.dialog.sending.description: Label on the submit button while the feedback is being sent (button is disabled meanwhile). Ends with a single-character ellipsis (…). Keep it short.

feedback.sentToast: Thanks for the feedback! We read every note.

@feedback.sentToast.description: Success toast shown briefly after feedback is sent. Warm and appreciative.
